content:
The window is real. It’s also shorter than you think.

8 min read
Just now

--

Read the article for free here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

You are four years into your backend engineering career. You are looking at a job board on a Tuesday night, staring at a posting for an “Applied AI Engineer” at a company like Ramp or Stripe. The salary band is noticeably higher than a standard backend role, and the requirements list tools and protocols that barely existed two years ago. You do the mental math on whether to abandon your current, predictable trajectory to chase this new thing.
The hiring data backs up that instinct. AI Engineer roles grew 13x over two years, while Forward Deployed Engineer roles grew 42x. Those are the two fastest-growing tech titles anywhere. Over 1.3 million net-new AI jobs appeared globally (LinkedIn Jan 2026).
The title of this article is right that AI engineers are having a moment. It is wrong about how long the moment lasts.
The premium for this title is spreading fast. Enterprise software engineers using AI code assistants will hit 75% adoption by 2028 (Gartner). The window where “AI Engineer” means something special is 2026 to 2028 or 2029, not the full five years. The play is to aim for mid-level Builder positioning inside the next 12 to 18 months, and understanding why that…
